solutions.title: Tutorial
solutions.content:
1533C - Sweets
You have to simulate the process described in the statement. Let's consider one of the many approaches to the solution.
Let's store a boolean array $$$used$$$, where sweets will be marked (a sweet $$$i$$$ is marked if $$$used[i]$$$ is true) if Anya has already eaten them. As long as there is at least one index $$$i$$$, such that $$$s_i = 1$$$ and $$$used_i = false$$$, we choose the next sweet that Anya will eat. To find the next sweet, it is enough to run a loop that will find the $$$k$$$-th position from the previous one among the remaining sweets (i. e., those sweets whose values in $$$used$$$ are $$$false$$$).

solutions.title: Solution (Neon)
solutions.content:
fun main
()
=
repeat
(
readLine
()!!.
toInt
())
{
val
(
n
,
k
)
=
readLine
()!!.
split
(
" "
).
map
{
it
.
toInt
()
}
val s
=
readLine
()!!
val used
=
BooleanArray
(
n
)
{
false
}
var
cnt
=
s
.
count
({
it
==
'1'
})
var
pos
=
0
while
(
cnt
>
0
)
{
used
[
pos
]
=
true
if
(
s
[
pos
]
==
'1'
)
--
cnt
if
(
cnt
==
0
)
break
var
lft
=
k
while
(
lft
>
0
)
{
pos
=
(
pos
+
1
)
%
n
if
(!
used
[
pos
])
--
lft
;
}
}
println
(
used
.
count
({
it
}))
}
